title: coleogeton filiformis subsp. occidentalis
intro: Coleogeton filiformis subsp. occidentalis, also known as the Westland coleogeton, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the family Typhaceae. It is native to the Westland region of New Zealand's South Island. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which can help to soothe digestive issues and reduce the risk of infection. The plant can be prepared as a herbal tea, tincture, or infusion, which can be used to alleviate symptoms of digestive discomfort, and may also help to support immune function and overall well-being.
